events.name: Indigenous Peoples' Day / Parent Conference Day

calendarNotes:
Austin ISD begins the 2026-2027 school year on August 18, 2026 — later than the other major Texas urban districts: Houston ISD starts August 10, Dallas ISD starts August 11, and Northside ISD (San Antonio) starts August 10. Austin ISD's August 18 start is the latest of this peer group, giving families roughly one additional week of summer compared to Houston or Dallas. Spring Break runs March 15–19, 2027, and the school year ends May 27, 2027. Families moving to Austin from the Northeast or West Coast should plan for a mid-August start, well ahead of the national average.

Spring Break for Austin ISD falls directly during South by Southwest (SXSW), one of the country's largest music, film, and technology festivals. During this week, downtown hotel prices surge, restaurant waits double, and traffic on major corridors backs up significantly even for families with no connection to the festival. Families planning out-of-town travel during Spring Break often find that Austin is less competitive as a destination than usual — most of the crowd is arriving, not leaving. That said, all transportation hubs (airport, highways, rental cars) remain highly congested through the break period.

In addition to district-wide no-school days, individual campuses in Austin ISD schedule their own Professional Learning and Engagement Days (PLED Days), which are student holidays not always listed on the main district calendar. The September 21 and April 30 PLED Days listed on the district calendar are system-wide, but each campus may add additional site-specific early-release days. Contact your child's school office in late August to get a complete list of campus-level no-school days for the year.

Austin's population growth over the past decade has been among the fastest of any major Texas city, and attendance zone boundaries are revised periodically to reflect new school construction. Families who have recently moved — even a few blocks within Austin — should re-verify their assigned school directly with the district before the new school year. Do not rely on a neighbor's assignment or last year's boundary map. New schools have opened on Austin's eastern and northern corridors as development extends into areas that were previously outside the urban core.
